summary.intent: Implement a framework-agnostic SegmentGroup component (issue #232) in the fogodev/ars-ui repository, including a state machine, connect API, ARIA attributes, roving focus, hidden input handling, and controlled/uncontrolled value management.
summary.outcome: PR #702 was opened, Codex reviewed it and found 4 bugs, all 3 implementation bugs were fixed with regression tests added first, specs for Leptos and Dioxus were updated, and the fix commit was pushed.

repo: The repo uses `cargo insta` for snapshot testing; the `--unreferenced=reject` flag will fail if other components have pre-existing unreferenced snapshots unrelated to the current PR, making it unsuitable as a blanket gate for per-component test runs.
The `cargo xtask spec validate` command is part of the standard test suite and validates spec conformance separately from unit tests.
Spec files for framework adapters (Leptos, Dioxus) live under `spec/leptos-components/` and `spec/dioxus-components/` as markdown files and must be kept in sync with the core component API.
Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment; the bot signals pickup with an `eyes` reaction from `chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]`.
The `snapshot-reviewed` label is applied to PRs containing `.snap` fixture files.

code.finding: Bug site for absent-value selection not being rejected — the machine was not guarding against selecting a value that does not correspond to any registered item.
Two bugs co-located here: focused item not cleared on prop sync when the focused item's registered item was removed, and the disabled hidden input attribute being set as readonly instead of disabled.
Missing adapter-specific props absent from the Leptos spec; needed to match the core connect API surface.
Missing adapter-specific props absent from the Dioxus spec; needed to match the core connect API surface.

workflow: Adding failing regression tests before fixing bugs (test-first for bug fixes) was followed deliberately, providing a safety net and proof of each defect.
Polling for Codex review completion via `sleep 120 && gh api` (GraphQL checking reactions and unresolved threads) is viable but fragile — network failures after the sleep require full manual re-runs.
The `receiving-code-review` skill at `~/.claude/skills/receiving-code-review/SKILL.md` guides how to process and respond to review findings.
Fix commits attributing external reviewers use the `Co-Authored-By` trailer (e.g., `Codex <codex@openai.com>`).

friction: Sandboxed `sleep + gh api` polling commands repeatedly failed with network connection errors after the sleep delay, requiring multiple manual re-runs outside the sandbox for each polling round.
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` cannot be scoped safely to a single component — pre-existing unreferenced snapshots in other components cause spurious failures unrelated to the current PR.
open_items: Pre-existing unreferenced snapshots in other components need to be cleaned up so `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` can be used as a reliable repo-wide gate.
The sandboxed polling mechanism for Codex review completion is brittle; a more robust out-of-sandbox or webhook-driven approach should be considered for future PRs.
